title: ludwigia major
intro: Ludwigia major, also known as water primrose, is a semi-aquatic plant species native to the Americas and Africa, characterized by its ability to thrive in both aquatic and terrestrial environments. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ludwigia major are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Ludwigia major can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional African medicine to treat various ailments, including snake bites and skin conditions.
